questions.description: Jen operates an advice and support centre that helps small businesses in… Frustrat.
questions.text: Jen operates an advice and support centre that helps small businesses in pursuing claims for non-payment of debts. Over the years she has become increasingly frustrated with the attitude of people with regard to paying her clients what they are owed. As a result, in recent times, she has taken to putting up posters in the front window of the centre which contain pictures and details of individuals who have been forced to court to pay the debts owed to her clients. On the posters she has a large color picture of the individual, with the words PAY YOUR DEBTS, SCUMBAG! Below in smaller print there is more detail about the amount of money owed, to whom and for how long. Jen calls it the window of infamy. One of Jen's clients has accused Douglas of not paying what he owes to them. Douglas has disputed this, saying that he transferred the money electronically, although he admits that he may have provided incorrect bank details for Jen's client but that his bank has said it may take some time to establish where the money has gone to, if they are ever able to find out. Douglas has refused to deal with the issue, saying it is for the bank to sort out. Jen decides to post Douglas' picture in her window of infamy. One day, National Television is filming a piece on the impact of the pandemic for the evening news that night. When it is broadcast, the front of Jen's centre is clearly visible and in particular the picture of Douglas with PAY YOUR DEBTS, SCUMBAG! is clearly visible. Advise Douglas if he has any action in tort law only against either Jen or National Television.

questions.description: Maurice is a surgeon specializing in the removal of varicose veins. Advise Katherine if she has any action in tort…
questions.text: Maurice is a surgeon specializing in the removal of varicose veins. Katherine has been referred to Maurice for bad varicose veins on her left leg. After an examination, Maurice agreed that the varicose veins in her left leg were sufficiently bad that they needed to be surgically removed. He arranged an appointment for 6 weeks after this examination, as there was a waiting list for the operating theatre. When the day of the operation came, Katherine was wheeled into the operating theatre where she met Maurice who said to her "Now Katherine don't worry, we will take good care of you. You will be under general anesthetic so you will not feel anything. I will do whatever needs to be done for that leg of yours. Trust me. Is that Ok?" Katherine nodded and replied "Maurice, that's absolutely fine, I know you will only do what is necessary." As the anesthetic was being given to Katherine, Maurice began looking at her right leg. He had not remembered seeing any varicose veins on her examination but now it was clear that her right leg would soon be as bad as her left leg. He tried to talk to Katherine, but she was already unconscious. He began operating on Katherine's left leg and it went exceptionally well. At that point Maurice decided it would be a good idea to operate on her right leg as it would have to be operated on within a year in any event. The theatre nurse advised Maurice that they were only supposed to operate on her left leg, but Maurice had said that Katherine had said he could do whatever was necessary. The operation on both legs was a great success. However, Katherine was furious that Maurice had operated on leg without her consent. Advise Katherine if she has any action in tort against Maurice for the operation on her right leg.

questions.description: Alexa was an administrator working for WeHearYou Ltd., a company which… Company.
questions.text: Alexa was an administrator working for WeHearYou Ltd., a company which provided call centre services, when the Covid-19 pandemic resulted in the first lockdown and all employees were required to work from home. Every employee was given a specialised telephone and headset which enabled everyone to take customer calls from the comfort of their own home. Alexa set up her office in an upstairs bedroom and found that she was very productive. However, she noted that she was feeling pain in her lower back, which she put down to the use of a dining room chair while she was sitting at a dressing table where her laptop was located. She loved her chair back in the office but there was nothing to be done now. When the first lockdown ended, Alexa returned to working from the office and she emailed HR noting that if there was a second lockdown, she would like to take her office chair home with her. HR wrote back that they could not authorise that but in the event of a second lockdown the issue would be revisited. Six months later there was a second lockdown and all employees of WeHearYou Ltd were told to work from home. Alexa again asked HR could she take her office chair home with her and was told that as no decision had been made, she could not take the chair home at that point. About a week into the lockdown, Alexa suffered a severe spasm in her back as a result of poor posture and on the dining room chair, as she got up to walk it off, she tripped over trailing extension leads for her laptop and printer and broke her ankle. Advise WeHearYou Ltd what liability in tort law they might have to Alexa in these circumstances.

questions.description: Critically discuss how damages are calculated in a tort action, including… Damages.
questions.text: Critically discuss how damages are calculated in a tort action, including but not limited to situations where the tort results in the death of the plaintiff.

questions.description: John was working for a major finance company, MoneyBags Ltd. Company.
questions.text: John was working for a major finance company, MoneyBags Ltd. As John became more senior, he was given more authority to make financial trades online on his own and without approval. These trades involved buying and selling shares on behalf of clients and were worth many millions of euros. When the Covid-19 pandemic arose, John, like all his colleagues, was told to work from home and supplied with a secure laptop which was encrypted and had a secure internet link to the head office. In addition, MoneyBags Ltd provided instructions for working from home. Employees were to designate a room in their accommodation to which no one else was to have access, the computer was to be locked and password protected anytime it was left unattended and was not to be used by anyone other than the employee. John found working from home very difficult because the schools were closed, and his teenage son was at home at a loose end. His son would pound around the house, playing his music at a loud volume. As a result, John decided to use his bedroom to set up his laptop and work from there. One evening, John was called to the front door as one of his neighbours needed help. While he was doing this, his partner Saoirse was in their bedroom when she decided that she wanted to check on her FacePicture page. As she knew John's password she logged on and in doing so accidentally sold 1 million shares belonging to Shane for only 1 cent each, when they were worth 1 euro each. Advise Shane as to any action he might have in the law of tort on these facts.

questions.description: Critically discuss the distinction between public and private nuisance, referring to relevant case law.
questions.text: Critically discuss the distinction between public and private nuisance, referring to relevant case law.

questions.description: The local authority had established a small pitch and putt course for the… Advise Simon what action if any he might…
questions.text: The local authority had established a small pitch and putt course for the benefit of the local community. Over time however, it had become clear that there was insufficient interest from the local community in playing pitch and putt and the costs of running it were becoming excessive for the possible benefit to the community. As a result, the local authority decided to close the course while it decided what to do with the land. In closing the course, the local authority simply locked the gates but maintained no on-site security, relying instead upon An Garda Siochana and their patrols. As a result, the course had become attractive to young people who found themselves with nothing to do during the winter nights and they would often climb over the fence and play on the course themselves, followed by heavy drinking sessions and music raves. Many in the community were annoyed at the way the local authority had abandoned the course and ignored the anti-social behaviour that was constantly taking place. As a result, Simon, a local resident, decided to take matters into his own hands. He hid on the course and waited for the first sign of these young people to arrive. When they did so, he ran at them shouting, attempting to chase them off. Suddenly he fell into a big hole which the local authority had dug for survey purposes. The hole had been covered with rotten wooden boards and Simon had been so angry he had not spotted the traffic cones at each of the four corners of the hole. Advise Simon what action if any he might have against the local authority.

questions.description: Critically discuss the issue of concurrent wrongdoers in tort law, paying particular attention to relevant…
questions.text: Critically discuss the issue of concurrent wrongdoers in tort law, paying particular attention to relevant jurisprudence in the allocation of responsibility between wrongdoers.

questions.description: Critically discuss the law relating to liability in negligence for pure… Negligenc.
questions.text: Critically discuss the law relating to liability in negligence for pure economic loss.

questions.description: Jenny makes a skin care product from local, organically grown herbs and… Advise Jenny if she has any action under the…
questions.text: Jenny makes a skin care product from local, organically grown herbs and flowers, which she calls Organic Skin Refresher. It is packaged in a pale green eco-friendly corrugated cardboard container. While Jenny wants to keep her business small and local, she is delighted that her product has been selected for sale in her local Megastore, a large retail chain with several branches nationwide. The idea is to showcase local producers in the locality where the store is located. Her product is highly successful and although she has been asked to make additional products such as hair shampoos and conditioners, she wants to remain small and local. About six months after her products were placed on sale in Megastore, she noticed that right next to her products, Megastore have introduced a new range of hair care products under the name Organic Refresher. It uses the same pale green colour as her product and although the packaging looks identical to her eco-friendly corrugated cardboard, it is in fact made of non-recyclable plastic. It is on sale nationwide in all Megastores. Jenny is horrified that this product will be confused with her organic product. Advise Jenny if she has any action under the law relating to passing off against Megastore.

questions.description: Critically discuss the time limitation of actions relating to latent defects and suggest any reforms that you think…
questions.text: Critically discuss the time limitation of actions relating to latent defects and suggest any reforms that you think might be needed in this area.

questions.description: Frank was driving along the road when he came across road works.
questions.text: Frank was driving along the road when he came across road works. The signage was confusing and some of the bollards that had been put on the road to guide road users into the correct path have been blown into the wrong position by passing lorries. This should have been monitored by the road construction firm. As a result, Frank entered the wrong lane and as he continued down the road, he realised his mistake. Spotting an opportunity to correct his position, he began crossing over lanes to get back to where he should be. On one piece of the road where he was changing lanes, the road construction company had failed to put in a concrete barrier to prevent anybody entering it. As Frank went on to that section of the road, he got a puncture, which caused his front wheel to deflate very quickly. As his car ground to a halt in the wrong lane he was hit by Mary he was driving in her correct lane but had been distracted by her three children in the back seat of the car. Advised Frank who might be the cause of the accident in any tort action by Mary.

questions.description: Hilary is an alternative therapist who specialises in dealing with various… Advise Bill as to any action for an…
questions.text: Hilary is an alternative therapist who specialises in dealing with various different anxiety and depression issues using unconventional treatments. In particular, she uses what is known as an isolation room that is basically an empty room that is kept in complete and utter darkness. The idea simply put is to remove all external stimuli other than white noise. That sound is played at a volume that is barely perceptible to the human ear. Bill is a patient of Hilary. He is not responding well to the standard treatments for anxiety and depression. As a result, Hilary suggests that he try the isolation room. Bill is concerned that he will feel claustrophobic, but Hilary reassures him that he can ask to be released at any point. Bill agrees to try the isolation room. When Bill is in the room, Hilary starts the white noise. Bill begins to feel sick, and it later transpires that this is not an uncommon side effect of using white noise at that particular volume. Hilary was aware of this but had decided not to tell Bill since she believed the benefits outweighed the risks. Bill now starts shouting that he wishes to leave the room, but Hilary tells him that she will not release him until the time is over. After about 30 minutes, Hilary realizes that it is not working and lets Bill out. Advise Bill as to any action for an intentional tort that he may have against Hilary.

questions.description: Daily newspaper has published an op-ed piece by Gerry, about Eddie, a…
questions.text: Daily newspaper has published an op-ed piece by Gerry, about Eddie, a rural politician in Ireland. In the piece, Gerry has written the following: In my opinion - and this is just that one man's opinion - Eddie is unfit to be elected to government. In my opinion, Eddie appears to believe that Covid is not real, that the vaccinations are in attempt to microchip the population and that it is part of a new world order. Now Eddie has never said that, and to be fair it would be a valid viewpoint held by many people whom I admire and respect. My problem is that Eddie has never denied or accepted these allegations. In my opinion he should have the courage of his convictions and tell us all what he thinks. Otherwise, we are left with no option but to speculate. Now some might say that speculation is strengthened by the fact that he constantly attends demonstrations against the vaccination program, but I would not. I simply asked that he comes clean to the public he expects to vote for him. Eddie is shocked and horrified when he reads this. While it is true that he has attended anti-vaccination demonstrations, it has always been to argue for the vaccination program and debunk the microchip claim. He does, however, believe that the time has come for a single government for the world. Eddie further states that he has never been asked about the claims that Gerry is making. As a result, he has never been in a position to either deny or accept these allegations. Advise Eddie if he has any action in tort against Gerry for this op-ed piece and any defenses that may be available to Gerry.

questions.description: Molly is a senior manager with a large accountancy firm where she… Frustrat.
questions.text: Molly is a senior manager with a large accountancy firm where she supervises over 30 people. As the workload has expanded, Molly has become very stressed and many of her workers feel that she is aggressive and rude to them. One day Molly is in the middle of the office when one of her colleagues, Henry, starts arguing with her. Molly is furious at being humiliated in this way in front of her other colleagues. Finally, in a fit of anger Molly began shouting and screaming at Henry letting all the frustrations of the past erupt. Suddenly, Henry starts to clutch his chest and drops to the floor. All of Molly's colleagues gather round and start telling her that she has given Henry a heart attack. Molly is sick to her core and soon finds herself getting dizzy. She hears someone say Henry is dead. Molly now faints. When Molly wakes up, everyone is laughing, telling her that it was a practical joke to teach her a lesson. Henry says if she does not change her behavior, he could see someone having a real heart attack. Molly now cannot sleep at night, suffers panic attacks and finds she is unable to do her job. Advise Molly what action in tort, if any, she might have with respect to this situation.

questions.description: Critically discuss the liability in tort law for the acts of volunteers.
questions.text: Critically discuss the liability in tort law for the acts of volunteers.
